title: Myanmaria calycina

intro: Myanmaria calycina, also known as Myanmaria, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Myanmaria calycina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
